content: WebRTC latency debugging: chrome://webrtc-internals is the primary built-in tool — shows packet loss, delay, jitter, ICE state. Data channel labels appear in this UI, so descriptive labels matter. Third-party: TestRTC, Callstats.io, Wireshark for deeper analysis. For game overlays: developer must manually compute phone-timestamp-to-render-timestamp delta (requires synchronized clocks or round-trip estimation). Unreliable channels avoid head-of-line blocking but make jitter measurement harder since gaps appear. Best practice: include sequence number + timestamp in every sensor packet; receiver computes latency delta and jitter from these. A debug overlay showing rolling avg latency, packet loss %, and jitter ms is expected by game developers on any real-time input platform.
